official_functions.role: Construct the official Play clientbound move_vehicle packet fixture
Write, decode, and observe the official move_vehicle body
Name the packet id assigned in Play clientbound state

invariants: The official Play clientbound table assigns move_vehicle to packet id 0x39.
The official Play clientbound protocol codec dispatches the move_vehicle id to ClientboundMoveVehiclePacket.
The official move_vehicle body is position Vec3 as three doubles, yRot float, and xRot float.
The fixture uses primitive constructor values and does not require initialized Entity, vehicle, Level, or game state.

The framed bytes, packet table id, body bytes, decoded packet class, decoded fields, Play clientbound packet table, and remaining decode byte counts are generated by the official jar-backed answer artifact.
This contract does not prove vehicle existence, movement semantics, initialized Level state, render readiness, or client-load completion.
test_topology_note: rust_test_target names the current case adapter only; the manifest owns the runnable test topology.
stop_boundary: Do not use this framed dispatch/decode contract to infer vehicle existence, movement semantics, initialized Level state, render readiness, or client-load completion.
